tiers.2: On-demand peptide engine — verified, graduates into Tier 1

lines.name: Peptides
lines.tagline: Evidence-graded peptide protocols by clinical axis

protocols.name: AOD-9604
protocols.mechanism: GH fragment (176-191) — stimulates lipolysis without affecting blood glucose or IGF-1
protocols.primary_use: Adjunct fat-loss support in metabolic/weight programs
protocols.contraindications: pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Mild effect, clean metabolic profile — no IGF-1 or glucose impact. Best as an adjunct, not a standalone. Human weight-loss data is weak; set expectations low.

protocols.name: BPC-157
protocols.mechanism: Pentadecapeptide — upregulates VEGFR2/angiogenesis + NO pathway, promotes fibroblast migration
protocols.primary_use: Soft-tissue/tendon/ligament injury and GI mucosal repair, plateaued on standard care
protocols.contraindications: active/prior malignancy, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Image the injury first — don't use it to dodge a surgical lesion. Short goal-bound courses only; cancer history is an absolute no given the angiogenic mechanism.

protocols.name: Cerebrolysin
protocols.mechanism: Porcine brain-derived neuropeptide mix — neurotrophic (BDNF/NGF-like) and neuroprotective activity
protocols.primary_use: Cognitive support in post-stroke, TBI, and neurodegenerative decline
protocols.contraindications: pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity, severe renal impairment, status epilepticus
protocols.clinical_notes: EU/RU/AR-labeled, no US brand — fully off-shore. Given as IV/IM courses, not a quick injection. Epilepsy is a caution. Verify a legitimate source — counterfeits exist.

protocols.name: CJC-1295
protocols.mechanism: Long-acting GHRH analog — sustained stimulation of pituitary GH release
protocols.primary_use: GH-axis support paired with a ghrelin agonist for recovery and body composition
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, untreated hypothyroidism, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Default here is no-DAC (mod GRF 1-29), dosed daily; the DAC version is weekly. Almost always paired with ipamorelin. Watch fluid retention at higher doses.

protocols.name: CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin (blend)
protocols.mechanism: GHRH analog + ghrelin agonist — dual-pathway amplification of pulsatile GH release
protocols.primary_use: Combined GH-axis support for recovery, body composition, and sleep
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, untreated hypothyroidism, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: The workhorse growth combo — GHRH + GHRP synergy beats either alone. One bedtime shot. Watch fluid retention and glucose as you titrate.

protocols.name: Delta sleep-inducing peptide (DSIP)
protocols.mechanism: Nonapeptide — modulates sleep architecture and stress/neuroendocrine response
protocols.primary_use: Sleep-onset/quality support, adjunct in stress-related insomnia
protocols.contraindications: pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Inconsistent human data; effects are variable patient-to-patient. Bedtime SC dosing. Address sleep hygiene and OSA first — don't paper over an untreated disorder.

protocols.name: Epitalon
protocols.mechanism: Tetrapeptide — proposed telomerase activation and pineal/melatonin-axis modulation
protocols.primary_use: Longevity/sleep-axis support, short pulsed courses
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Human evidence is thin and mostly older Russian work. Dosed in short 10-20 day pulses, not chronically. Telomerase claims are unproven — set expectations honestly.

protocols.name: Copper tripeptide-1 (GHK-Cu)
protocols.mechanism: Copper-binding tripeptide — modulates wound-healing genes, collagen synthesis, angiogenesis
protocols.primary_use: Skin/connective-tissue repair, hair, and aesthetic regeneration
protocols.contraindications: active/prior malignancy, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Most evidence is topical; injectable/SC use is empiric. Watch cumulative copper with chronic systemic dosing. Same angiogenic cancer caution.

protocols.name: Ipamorelin
protocols.mechanism: Selective ghrelin/GHSR agonist — stimulates GH pulse with minimal cortisol/prolactin effect
protocols.primary_use: GH-axis support for recovery, body composition, and sleep, paired with a GHRH analog
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, untreated hypothyroidism, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Cleanest GHRP — minimal hunger/cortisol vs GHRP-6. Synergistic with CJC-1295 or sermorelin. Bedtime dosing leverages the natural GH pulse.

protocols.name: Kisspeptin-10
protocols.mechanism: KISS1R agonist — stimulates hypothalamic GnRH release, upstream of LH/FSH
protocols.primary_use: Libido and reproductive-axis support; investigational
protocols.contraindications: active hormone-sensitive cancer, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Very early human data, mostly IV research dosing — SC use is empiric. Acts upstream on GnRH. Caution with hormone-sensitive conditions; consent as experimental.

protocols.name: KPV
protocols.mechanism: α-MSH C-terminal tripeptide — anti-inflammatory via NF-κB downregulation
protocols.primary_use: Anti-inflammatory support, gut/skin inflammation adjunct
protocols.contraindications: transplant on immunosuppression, severe active autoimmune, pregnancy, lactation, age <18
protocols.clinical_notes: Anti-inflammatory tripeptide, oral or SC. Useful gut/skin adjunct; preclinical evidence. Pairs with Tα1 for an immune-axis combo. Not a substitute for IBD work-up.

protocols.name: MOTS-c
protocols.mechanism: Mitochondrial-derived peptide — activates AMPK, improves metabolic flexibility (exercise mimetic)
protocols.primary_use: Metabolic/healthspan support — insulin sensitivity and metabolic flexibility
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: No human therapeutic trials — preclinical only. Tie use to a number (HOMA-IR/A1c) and stop if it doesn't move. Exercise is the proven version of this mechanism.

protocols.name: Bremelanotide
protocols.mechanism: Melanocortin (MC4R) agonist — central activation of sexual desire/arousal pathways
protocols.primary_use: HSDD in premenopausal women (on-label); off-label ED/low desire
protocols.contraindications: uncontrolled HTN, significant CVD, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Central, not vascular — use when desire is the problem or PDE5 fails. Set the nausea expectation up front. Respect the BP contraindication; warn about hyperpigmentation.

protocols.name: Retatrutide
protocols.mechanism: Triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon receptor agonist — satiety + insulin sensitization + energy expenditure
protocols.primary_use: Investigational obesity/metabolic — severe obesity not at goal on dual agonists
protocols.contraindications: MTC/MEN-2 hx, acute pancreatitis, severe gastroparesis, pregnancy, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Not FDA-approved — Phase 3 ongoing. Glucagon arm can raise heart rate and nudge glucose; monitor closely. Treat as experimental and consent accordingly.

protocols.name: Selank
protocols.mechanism: Tuftsin analog — anxiolytic via GABA/serotonin modulation and BDNF, immunomodulatory
protocols.primary_use: Anxiety/stress and cognitive support without sedation
protocols.contraindications: pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Russian-origin anxiolytic peptide, dosed SC here, non-sedating. Pairs with Semax. SC gives tighter dose control than nasal; not a substitute for evaluating real anxiety.

protocols.name: Semax
protocols.mechanism: ACTH(4-10) analog — modulates BDNF and monoaminergic tone, neuroprotective
protocols.primary_use: Focus, cognitive support, and recovery from cognitive fatigue
protocols.contraindications: pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Russian-origin nootropic, now dosed SC here. Short well-tolerated courses; effects are subtle. SC gives more reliable dosing than intranasal drops — verify vial concentration.

protocols.name: Sermorelin
protocols.mechanism: GHRH(1-29) analog — stimulates endogenous pulsatile GH release via pituitary GHRH receptor
protocols.primary_use: Adult age-related GH decline with low-normal IGF-1 and consistent symptoms
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, untreated hypothyroidism, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Brand discontinued — compounded only. Treat thyroid first or you'll mislabel a responder. Bedtime, empty stomach; chase IGF-1 upper-normal-for-age, not above.

protocols.name: Elamipretide (SS-31)
protocols.mechanism: Cardiolipin-binding mitochondrial peptide — stabilizes inner membrane, improves ATP/electron transport
protocols.primary_use: Mitochondrial-dysfunction/fatigue and healthspan support
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Investigational; trials in mitochondrial myopathy mixed. Trial dose 40 mg SC; compounded longevity use is lower and empiric. Watch injection-site reactions.

protocols.name: Thymosin beta-4 (TB-500)
protocols.mechanism: Actin-binding peptide — promotes cell migration, angiogenesis, and tissue regeneration
protocols.primary_use: Soft-tissue/tendon injury and muscle recovery, adjunct to rehab
protocols.contraindications: active/prior malignancy, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Pairs with BPC-157 for the classic repair stack. Same angiogenic cancer caution applies. Loading phase twice weekly, then taper to maintenance.

protocols.name: Tesamorelin
protocols.mechanism: Stabilized GHRH analog — stimulates GH release, reduces visceral adipose tissue
protocols.primary_use: Excess visceral adiposity (on-label: HIV lipodystrophy); off-label VAT reduction
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, untreated hypothyroidism, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Strongest GH-secretagogue data for visceral fat. Brand is pricey; compounded common but verify source. Monitor IGF-1 and glucose — it can nudge insulin resistance.

protocols.name: Thymosin alpha-1
protocols.mechanism: 28-aa thymic peptide — TLR-2/9-mediated T-cell maturation and Th1 immune modulation
protocols.primary_use: Immune modulation in chronic/recurrent infection or immunosenescence
protocols.contraindications: transplant on immunosuppression, severe active autoimmune, pregnancy, lactation, age <18
protocols.clinical_notes: Modulator, not a generic booster — use against a defined immune problem. Always ask about transplant/immunosuppression. Strongest data is in hepatitis; favorable safety profile.

protocols.name: Tirzepatide
protocols.mechanism: Dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist — central satiety + insulin sensitization
protocols.primary_use: Type 2 diabetes and chronic weight management (BMI ≥30, or ≥27 with comorbidity)
protocols.contraindications: MTC/MEN-2 hx, acute pancreatitis, severe gastroparesis, pregnancy, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Titrate to tolerance, not by calendar — most 'non-response' is stopping at 5 mg. Cut sulfonylurea/insulin at start to avoid lows. Counsel on oral-contraceptive absorption.

protocols.name: Cognition Stack (Selank + Semax + DSIP + Cerebrolysin)
protocols.mechanism: Neuro stack — anxiolytic (Selank) + focus/BDNF (Semax) + sleep (DSIP) + neurotrophic (Cerebrolysin)
protocols.primary_use: Cognitive performance, stress, and sleep optimization
protocols.contraindications: pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity, severe renal impairment, uncontrolled epilepsy
protocols.clinical_notes: Cerebrolysin is the heavyweight here (IV/IM courses) and brings the renal/seizure cautions. SC peptides are subtle. Verify source authenticity for Cerebrolysin and Russian peptides.

protocols.name: Growth Stack (Sermorelin + Ipamorelin)
protocols.mechanism: GH-axis pair — GHRH analog (Sermorelin) + ghrelin/GHSR agonist (Ipamorelin) for synergistic pulsatile GH
protocols.primary_use: Recovery, body composition, and sleep in symptomatic low-normal IGF-1 adults
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, untreated hypothyroidism, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: One GHRH + one GHRP is the clean synergy — no need to stack two GHRH analogs. Treat thyroid first. One bedtime shot; keep IGF-1 upper-normal-for-age, not above.

protocols.name: Immunity Stack (Thymosin Alpha-1 + KPV)
protocols.mechanism: Immune stack — T-cell maturation/Th1 modulation (Tα1) + NF-κB anti-inflammatory (KPV)
protocols.primary_use: Immune modulation plus anti-inflammatory support
protocols.contraindications: transplant on immunosuppression, severe active autoimmune, pregnancy, lactation, age <18
protocols.clinical_notes: Tα1 sets immune tone, KPV calms inflammation — complementary. Always screen for transplant/immunosuppression and autoimmune disease before starting either.

protocols.name: Longevity Stack (Epitalon + MOTS-c + SS-31)
protocols.mechanism: Longevity stack — telomerase/pineal (Epitalon) + AMPK (MOTS-c) + mitochondrial membrane (SS-31)
protocols.primary_use: Healthspan, metabolic, and mitochondrial support
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: All three are early-evidence — honest expectations required. Epitalon pulses, MOTS-c and SS-31 daily/weekly. Tie continuation to objective metabolic markers.

protocols.name: Metabolic Stack (Tesamorelin + CJC/Ipa Blend + AOD-9604 + MOTS-c)
protocols.mechanism: Metabolic stack — GHRH analogs (VAT reduction) + GH fragment lipolysis + AMPK metabolic flexibility
protocols.primary_use: Visceral fat reduction and metabolic optimization
protocols.contraindications: active malignancy, untreated hypothyroidism, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: Four agents — heavy stack; reserve for motivated patients with objective VAT. Tesamorelin and MOTS-c can both touch glucose, so monitor A1c. Diet/exercise still does the heavy lifting.

protocols.name: Vitality Stack (PT-141 + Kisspeptin-10)
protocols.mechanism: Vitality stack — central melanocortin arousal (PT-141) + upstream GnRH/hormone axis (Kisspeptin)
protocols.primary_use: Libido and sexual-function support — central + hormonal axes
protocols.contraindications: uncontrolled HTN, significant CVD, hormone-sensitive cancer, pregnancy, lactation, age <18
protocols.clinical_notes: PT-141 brings the BP contraindication and nausea; Kisspeptin is experimental and acts upstream. Document controlled BP and hormone-sensitive cancer screen before starting.

protocols.name: Wolverine GLOW (BPC-157 + TB-500 + GHK-Cu)
protocols.mechanism: Tri-peptide repair stack — angiogenesis (VEGFR2) + cell migration + collagen/wound-healing genes
protocols.primary_use: Aggressive soft-tissue/tendon repair and recovery, plateaued on standard care
protocols.contraindications: active/prior malignancy, copper overload, pregnancy, lactation, age <18, hypersensitivity
protocols.clinical_notes: The flagship repair combo — all three are angiogenic, so cancer history is an absolute no. Image the injury first. Short goal-bound course; watch cumulative copper from GHK-Cu.
